pdg_node.statement: (L_feature_min_max: array-like numeric bounds, R_features_distance: array-like numeric, interaction_distance: float) -> bool
pdg_node.informal_desc: Evaluates whether a candidate left/right feature pair satisfies interaction-distance constraints.

declaration.name: pair_distance_compatibility_check
declaration.type_signature: (L_feature_min_max: array-like numeric bounds, R_features_distance: array-like numeric, interaction_distance: float) -> bool
declaration.docstring: Evaluates whether a candidate left/right feature pair satisfies interaction-distance constraints.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Interval-Constrained Pairwise Metric Predicate. This operation performs a feasibility test over pairwise metric relationships by comparing candidate distance values against a bounded admissible interval and a non-negative reference distance. It maps numeric distance descriptors from two entities into a single Boolean decision indicating whether the pair is geometrically compatible under the specified constraints. Conceptually, it is a constraint-satisfaction projection from continuous metric space to a binary validity state.. Applications: Robotics: validating whether two manipulator waypoints can be connected under kinematic separation bounds, Computational biology: checking whether candidate residue pairs satisfy allowable spatial separation ranges in structural modeling, Wireless systems: testing whether transmitter-receiver candidate links meet admissible range constraints for connectivity, 3D computer vision: screening feature correspondences by verifying epipolar or depth-consistent distance envelopes

pdg_node.statement: (edges: list of weighted edge tuples, nodes: list/set of node identifiers) -> networkx.Graph or networkx.DiGraph
pdg_node.informal_desc: Constructs the final weighted NetworkX graph from derived nodes and edges.

declaration.name: networkx_weighted_graph_materialization
declaration.type_signature: (edges: list of weighted edge tuples, nodes: list/set of node identifiers) -> networkx.Graph or networkx.DiGraph
declaration.docstring: Constructs the final weighted NetworkX graph from derived nodes and edges.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Weighted Relational Topology Materialization. Transforms two finite collections—entity identifiers and weighted pairwise relations—into an explicit adjacency-based topology object. The procedure first materializes the vertex set, then inserts each relation as an edge annotated by a scalar weight, preserving the provided connectivity pattern. It performs structural encoding rather than optimization or inference, yielding a directly queryable weighted graph representation.. Applications: Neuroscience: materializing weighted synaptic connectivity maps between recorded neural units, Infrastructure engineering: constructing weighted utility or transport connectivity networks from link measurements, Computational biology: encoding weighted interaction topologies among molecular entities, Distributed systems: representing weighted communication latency graphs between compute nodes
